concerns.text: Task T013 instructs aborting with [POWER_INSUFFICIENT] if N < 92. Spec FR-001 explicitly requires the system to NOT abort in this case, but instead generate a report with a warning and continue. This is a missing/incorrect task for the FR-001 requirement.

Task T014 mandates an abort with [ETHICAL_GAP] if well-being protocols are missing. Spec FR-017 only requires verifying randomization and defaulting to 'associational' if metadata is missing; the spec contains no requirement to abort the pipeline for missing ethical metadata. The task does not cover the FR-017 requirement correctly.

Task T031 applies Holm-Bonferroni across '4 hypothesis tests' (3 primary + 1 robustness). Spec FR-011b requires applying correction SEPARATELY to the robustness tests from the primary tests. The task fails to cover the specific requirement of separate correction families.

Task T019 describes applying Holm-Bonferroni across 'primary outcomes and robustness moderator' as a single group. This contradicts spec FR-011 (primary only) and FR-011b (robustness only) which define two separate correction families. The task does not cover the FR-011 requirement correctly.

Task T030 implements bootstrap logic but does not explicitly include the instruction to emit the specific caveat string '[BOOTSTRAP_CONVERGENCE_CAVEAT: ...]' if the cap is reached, as required by FR-008. The task description is incomplete for the spec requirement.

Task T013 lists 'scf_total' as a required column. Spec FR-001 requires 'scf_total' but the context implies standard SCS naming; however, the task description's column list must match the spec exactly to avoid validation failures. The task description is ambiguous regarding the exact column name required by FR-001.
